description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a military-style olive green fuselage and dual wings, each featuring roundel insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft. The upper and lower wings are connected by wooden struts, and the front of the plane is dominated by a large, exposed radial engine with a wooden propeller. The tail section is painted with red, white, and blue stripes, and the vertical stabilizer is conventional in design. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, likely designed for a single pilot, with minimal canopy or windscreen protection.
description: A vintage olive green biplane with dual wings, exposed radial engine, wooden propeller, and open single-seat cockpit. Features military roundels and a striped tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large radial engine, wooden propeller, and dual wings with roundels; landing gear and struts are clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open cockpit, tail with red, white, and blue stripes, and the stacked biplane wing structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the dual wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit opening, wing struts, and the detailed tail section with its colorful stripes.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; classic rudder and elevator surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine, with minimal windscreen.
